name: Sourdough Starter
recipeInstructions:
1. Pour the water into a crock or wide-mouth gallon jar.
2. Pour in the yeast and let it dissolve.
3. Stir, then add vinegar, sugar, and flour.
4. Mix well and cover with a cloth.
5. Set the mixture in a warm place to sour for 2-3 days.
6. When activity stops and the mixture flattens out, an amber-colored liquor will form on top, and it will have a distinct smell.
7. Mix everything together until it looks like whipped cream.
8. Transfer the starter to a glass jar with a screw-type lid and place in the refrigerator.
9. The sourdough starter will keep for months and continue to improve as it ages slowly.
Growing better over time, this recipe is great for pancakes, waffles, coffee cake, rolled biscuits, quick drop biscuits, cinnamon rolls, cobblers, bread, and even oatmeal cookies. Experiment and enjoy the tasty results!
